text: The surgeon performs a single-port ileocolic resection with mesenterectomy, beginning by creating a submesenteric tunnel. The lateral peritoneal attachments are then dissected to achieve lateral mobilization. The proximal transaction line is marked, and the ileocolic trunk is identified to ensure it is spared during the extended mesenterectomy. A mesenteric window is created once the position of the ileocolic trunk is confirmed.

The surgeon dissects the mesentery from the ileocolic trunk, progressing towards the proximal transaction point. The dissection continues alongside the ileocolic trunk towards the colonic transaction point, revealing a triangular-shaped mesentery.
The surgeon closes the mesenteric defect using clips after completing the extracorporeal fashioning of the anastomosis.
